research_disposition.decisive_next_evidence: quality-controlled multi-scale measurements with declared cost boundary, operator hours, spend, behavioural similarity, graph independence, survival, correlated loss, replacement and achieved effect

properties_exercised: unit and attack tests including R011 scenario, sensitivity and adversarial assumptions

nexus_workflow_observations.context_reconstruction_source: repository task, route, evidence, model, tests and receipts
nexus_workflow_observations.administrative_cost_observation: The workflow produced useful external research artifacts, but a single slice cannot yet establish whether the governance overhead is proportionate across repeated tasks.

demonstrated: The repository can carry a bounded real research question from task contract through evidence register, competing model, executable artifact, tests, adversarial review and decision brief on a proposal branch.
The implementation represents and tests linear, sublinear, superlinear and threshold/correlation-driven regime scenarios.
The evidence boundary survived synthesis: the leading regime-dependent interpretation remains labelled INFERRED rather than promoted to empirical fact.
The branch can be reconstructed without relying on chat memory for its task, evidence, assumptions, outputs and unresolved questions.
limitations: The public literature does not provide a quality-controlled dataset from which to estimate a universal scaling exponent.
The scenario parameters are assumptions, not fitted empirical estimates.
Detection datasets are selection-biased toward observed or disrupted operations.
One completed vertical slice does not establish general Nexus effectiveness.
A successful workflow proves only the exercised repository properties and does not establish complete correctness, security, privacy or scientific validity.
Canonical promotion requires explicit user approval and a final green pull-request head.
